vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1498: Substrate fix (PAN-1454 pattern 1): require live-code-path trace in requirements review

plan.title: Substrate fix (PAN-1454 pattern 1): require live-code-path trace in requirements review
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The requirements reviewer (post-PAN-1217 scope-bucket work) classifies each AC by scope, but it can still mark an AC `Implemented (in_pr_scope)` without ever identifying the file/function that produces the user-visible behavior. Pattern 1 from the PAN-1454 audit — code lands but in the wrong file or wrong code path — slips straight through because the substrate accepts the reviewer's word that 'something was changed' without forcing the reviewer to name what. Audit examples PAN-1122 (trimmed a static catalog while the UI reads from a server endpoint), PAN-1408 (gitignored target so change never applied), and PAN-1194 (UI affordances missing) all passed requirements review with this gap.
narratives.Proposal: Make the substrate enforce a per-AC `Live Code Path Trace` deterministically — not through prompt politeness. (1) Extend `roles/review-requirements.md` so the reviewer MUST emit a `## Live Code Path Traces` section with a `### AC: <title>` block per in-PR-scope Implemented/Partial AC, each containing a `**File:** \`path:line\``, `**Function:**`, and `**Path:**` line. (2) Implement a pure TypeScript validator that parses the reviewer's markdown and confirms every required AC has a trace whose `**File:**` value is a backticked path with extension and line number. (3) Wire that validator into the requirements sub-role launcher: on claude exit, run the validator, and signal `REVIEWER_READY` only on validator pass — on fail signal `REVIEWER_FAILED requirements missing live code path trace for ACs: <list>`. (4) The synthesis agent already treats REVIEWER_FAILED as blocking (roles/review.md §step 7), so the review surfaces as 'changes requested' with a specific, actionable reason — no LLM judgment in the enforcement loop. Verifying the cited path is the test role's job and remains out of scope here (per the issue).

autoDecisions.summary: Enforcement lives at the launcher signal boundary, not as synthesis-prompt language.
autoDecisions.rationale: The whole point of a substrate fix for the silent-miss pattern is to remove the LLM from the enforcement loop. Adding instructions to the synthesis prompt would re-create the same class of bug — a reviewer ignoring the requirement. A deterministic parser at the signal boundary is the only way the contract is mechanically enforced.

autoDecisions.summary: Trace required only on `in_pr_scope` ACs marked Implemented or Partial.
autoDecisions.rationale: Whole-feature-scope and pre-existing ACs have no live code path inside the PR diff; demanding one would force the reviewer to fabricate evidence (which is the very failure mode we are blocking). Missing/Not-applicable ACs trivially have nothing to point at. Limiting the requirement to in_pr_scope Implemented/Partial matches the audit examples PAN-1122/1408/1194 (all in-PR-scope claims of coverage) and avoids re-opening the pre-existing-noise problem PAN-1217 closed.

autoDecisions.summary: Canonical trace format uses three bolded fields (`**File:**`, `**Function:**`, `**Path:**`) inside one `### AC:` block per AC, in a single `## Live Code Path Traces` section.
autoDecisions.rationale: Bolded-field-on-line shape is what review-requirements.md already uses for findings (`**Scope:**`, `**Expected:**`, etc.), so the reviewer LLM produces it reliably. The single section grouping makes the parser O(N) over one section instead of having to walk every Findings block. Backticked `path:line` matches the Evidence column convention already in the reviewer output.

autoDecisions.summary: CLI shim is `pan review validate-trace <file>` (under the existing `pan review` command group), exit 0 = pass, exit 1 + one-line stderr = fail.
autoDecisions.rationale: The launcher script is bash; needing a CLI seam is the simplest way to invoke TypeScript validation from inside the generated launcher. `pan review` already exists for review subcommands (review-spawn-reviewer.ts). The shim is a thin parseArgs + call + exit-code wrapper, fully covered by the validator's own unit tests.

autoDecisions.summary: When the validator CLI is unavailable on a remote worker (e.g. stale binary), the launcher falls back to the legacy file-present signal and writes a one-line activity-log warning instead of failing every requirements review.
autoDecisions.rationale: Hard-failing on missing tooling would silently break review across stale workers. The fallback is observable (one warning per affected run) so the operator can upgrade, but doesn't strand work. This is the only acceptable bandaid because the alternative is worse.

items.title: Extend review-requirements.md to require a per-AC live code path trace section

narrative.Action:
Edit roles/review-requirements.md to add a new mandatory output section '## Live Code Path Traces'. For every AC the reviewer marks Implemented or Partial AND classified as Scope: in_pr_scope, the reviewer MUST emit a '### AC: <ac title>' block containing exactly these bolded-field lines:

  **Scope:** in_pr_scope
  **File:** `path/to/file.ts:42`
  **Function:** `functionName`
  **Path:** <one-paragraph description of how user input reaches this function>

The section MUST appear between '## Findings' and '## Non-blocking Notes'. The reviewer MUST also include the section even when there are zero in-PR-scope Implemented/Partial ACs — with the body 'None — no in_pr_scope ACs claimed Implemented or Partial.' so the parser can distinguish 'reviewer addressed the requirement' from 'reviewer forgot the section'.

Update the existing 'Output format' template in the same file. Make the requirement contractual: 'If this section is missing or any required AC trace omits a `**File:** \`path:line\`` line, the launcher will signal REVIEWER_FAILED — the synthesis agent will not see your report as passing.' Also drop one sentence into roles/review.md §step 6 noting that the requirements reviewer's output now includes a Live Code Path Traces section (so the synthesis agent can quote it in its synthesis report). Keep the change additive — do not remove existing sections.

subItems.title: roles/review-requirements.md contains a 'Live Code Path Traces' subsection inside the Output format block, with a verbatim example showing **File:**, **Function:**, and **Path:** lines

The prompt explicitly states that the section is required for every in_pr_scope Implemented/Partial AC, and that whole_feature_scope / pre_existing / Missing / Not-applicable ACs do NOT require a trace
The prompt explicitly tells the reviewer that the section MUST be present even with zero qualifying ACs, with the literal body 'None — no in_pr_scope ACs claimed Implemented or Partial.'

The prompt states that omitting the section or any required `**File:** \`path:line\`` line causes the launcher to signal REVIEWER_FAILED (so the reviewer understands enforcement is mechanical, not LLM-judged)

roles/review.md §6 Output template references the new Live Code Path Traces section as something the synthesis report should quote when summarizing requirements coverage; no existing sections are removed

Snapshot test (tests/unit covering roles/) asserts the presence of the 'Live Code Path Traces' header and the required field tokens (**File:**, **Function:**, **Path:**) in roles/review-requirements.md

items.title: Implement pure-function requirements-trace validator with fixture-driven unit tests

narrative.Action:
Add src/lib/cloister/review-requirements-validator.ts exporting:

  export interface RequirementsTraceValidationResult {
    ok: boolean;
    /** Titles of in_pr_scope Implemented/Partial ACs missing a usable trace */
    missingTraces: string[];
    /** Human-readable one-line reason for REVIEWER_FAILED; empty when ok */
    reason: string;
  }
  export function validateRequirementsTrace(markdown: string): RequirementsTraceValidationResult

The validator parses the reviewer's markdown:
  1. Walks the Coverage Matrix table to enumerate ACs and their (scope, status) tuple.
  2. Builds the required-trace set: ACs where status ∈ {Implemented, Partial} AND scope === 'in_pr_scope'.
  3. Locates the '## Live Code Path Traces' section (case-sensitive, anchored at line start).
  4. For each '### AC:' block under that section, extracts the **File:** line and validates that its value is a backticked token matching /^`\S+\.[A-Za-z0-9]+:\d+`$/ (path with extension and colon-line-number).
  5. Cross-references the trace blocks against the required-trace set. Any required AC that lacks a matching block, or has a block whose **File:** doesn't match the regex, goes into missingTraces.
  6. If the required-trace set is empty AND the section body is exactly the canonical 'None — no in_pr_scope ACs claimed Implemented or Partial.' sentinel, ok=true. If the required set is empty but the section is missing entirely, ok=false (because the substrate cannot distinguish 'reviewer addressed it' from 'reviewer forgot it').

The failure reason is 'requirements review missing live code path trace for ACs: <comma-separated titles>' (truncate to 240 chars to fit a `pan tell` payload comfortably).

Tests (src/lib/cloister/__tests__/review-requirements-validator.test.ts) use markdown fixtures under tests/fixtures/review-requirements/:
  - happy-path.md: two in_pr_scope Implemented ACs, both with valid traces → ok
  - whole-feature-only.md: one whole_feature_scope Implemented AC, sentinel body in traces section → ok
  - missing-section.md: section absent entirely → fail, reason names both ACs
  - missing-one-ac.md: two required, only one has a trace block → fail, reason names the missing one
  - bad-file-format.md: trace present but **File:** value is 'the dropdown' or a bare path with no extension/line → fail, reason names the AC
  - sentinel-when-not-needed.md: zero qualifying ACs, sentinel body present → ok
  - sentinel-when-needed.md: one qualifying AC but body is the sentinel → fail, reason names the AC
  - windows-path.md: trace **File:** value uses backslashes → fail (path regex rejects), reason names the AC
  - multiple-paths-one-block.md: a single block has two backticked paths, first valid → ok
  - case-variation.md: section header is '## live code path traces' lowercase → fail (case-sensitive contract)

The validator must be pure (no I/O, no process.cwd). The shim in bead 3 owns I/O.

subItems.title: validateRequirementsTrace exists with the signature above and is pure (no fs/exec)
All ten fixture cases above pass; each fixture lives as a separate .md file under tests/fixtures/review-requirements/ so future fixtures can be added without editing the test file
Failure reason starts with 'requirements review missing live code path trace for ACs:' and lists at least one AC title from the missingTraces array; reason is truncated to ≤240 chars

When the Coverage Matrix has zero in_pr_scope Implemented/Partial ACs AND the canonical sentinel body appears under '## Live Code Path Traces', the validator returns ok=true (this avoids blocking reviews that legitimately have nothing to trace)

When the Coverage Matrix has zero qualifying ACs but the section is missing entirely, the validator returns ok=false — the substrate cannot tell 'addressed' apart from 'forgotten' and must fail closed

The **File:** regex requires a non-empty path with at least one '.<ext>:<line>' suffix and rejects bare prose / Windows backslashes / missing line numbers (covered by bad-file-format.md and windows-path.md fixtures)

items.title: Add 'pan review validate-trace' CLI shim and wire it into the requirements sub-role launcher

narrative.Action:
Two parts, both in this bead because they only make sense shipped together:

(A) CLI shim — add src/cli/commands/review-validate-trace.ts implementing 'pan review validate-trace <outputFile>':
  - Reads outputFile from disk (fs/promises — never sync).
  - Calls validateRequirementsTrace(content).
  - On ok: process.exit(0).
  - On !ok: writes result.reason to stderr (one line, no chalk colors), process.exit(1).
  - Register the subcommand under the existing `pan review` group in src/cli/index.ts (alongside review-spawn-reviewer).
  - Unit test: invoke handler with both a passing fixture and a failing fixture, assert exit code and stderr output (use Vitest's process.exit / console mocking, not a real spawn).

(B) Launcher wiring — modify src/lib/launcher-generator.ts buildReviewSubRoleCommand so that when config.reviewSignal.subRole === 'requirements' the generated script becomes:

  echo $$ > <pidFile>
  timeout <N> <claude...>
  CLAUDE_EXIT=$?
  if [ "$CLAUDE_EXIT" = "124" ]; then
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_TIMEOUT requirements reviewer exceeded <N>s deadline" || true
  elif [ ! -s '<out>' ]; then
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_FAILED requirements reviewer exited (code $CLAUDE_EXIT) without writing report" || true
  elif command -v pan >/dev/null 2>&1 && pan review validate-trace '<out>' 2>/tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$; then
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_READY requirements <out>" || true
  elif [ -f /tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$ ]; then
    REASON=$(cat /tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$)
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_FAILED requirements $REASON" || true
  else
    # Validator CLI not installed on this worker — fallback to legacy file-present signal
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_READY requirements <out>" || true
    echo '[review-requirements] WARNING: pan review validate-trace unavailable on this worker — substrate trace check skipped' >&2
  fi
  rm -f /tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$
  touch <signalMarker>
  rm -f <pidFile>

The security/correctness/performance sub-roles MUST continue to use the existing buildReviewSubRoleCommand output byte-for-byte. Use a small branch (if subRole === 'requirements' return buildRequirementsLauncher(...); else return buildLegacyLauncher(...)) so the other three sub-roles' generated scripts are demonstrably unchanged.

Tests:
  - Extend src/lib/__tests__/launcher-generator.test.ts with two new cases: 'requirements sub-role generates validator-gated signal script' (snapshot/text-contains assertions for `pan review validate-trace`) and 'non-requirements sub-roles unchanged' (snapshot of security script identical to pre-change baseline).
  - The existing reviewSignal tests at lines 173 / 615 of that file stay green.

subItems.title: `pan review validate-trace <file>` exists as a subcommand under the `pan review` group, exits 0 on validator pass, exits 1 with the validator's one-line reason on stderr on fail
Unit test for the CLI shim exercises both pass and fail paths and asserts both exit code and stderr content

buildReviewSubRoleCommand emits a validator-gated signal sequence ONLY when reviewSignal.subRole === 'requirements'; the generated script invokes `pan review validate-trace` and signals REVIEWER_READY only on its exit-0
On validator failure the generated script signals `REVIEWER_FAILED requirements <reason>` with the validator's stderr verbatim (no LLM in the loop)

If `pan review validate-trace` is not on PATH on the worker, the script falls back to the legacy 'file present → REVIEWER_READY' contract and writes a single-line warning to stderr — proven by a launcher-generator test asserting the fallback branch exists
Launcher-generator snapshot tests for security/correctness/performance sub-roles remain byte-identical to the pre-change baseline (proves no regression in the other three reviewers)
No use of execSync / readFileSync / writeFileSync in the new CLI shim or any code path it imports — fs/promises and async exec only (per project rule)

items.title: End-to-end test: missing-trace requirements output produces REVIEWER_FAILED with the validator's reason

narrative.Action:
Add tests/unit/cloister/review-requirements-trace-e2e.test.ts that exercises the full signal contract without spawning a real claude:

  1. Generate a requirements sub-role launcher script via buildReviewSubRoleCommand (subRole: 'requirements', synthesisAgentId: 'agent-pan-XX-review', outputPath: <tmpdir>/requirements.md, etc.).
  2. Pre-place a missing-trace fixture markdown at outputPath.
  3. Run the generated script in a real bash shell against a stubbed `claude` that immediately exits 0 (so the script proceeds to the validator step) and a stubbed `pan` that captures the message it was told.
  4. Assert the captured `pan tell` argument starts with `REVIEWER_FAILED requirements requirements review missing live code path trace for ACs:`.
  5. Repeat with a happy-path fixture and assert `REVIEWER_READY requirements <out>`.

This closes the substrate contract end-to-end. Test should use real fs / real bash but stub out `claude` and `pan` via PATH manipulation in a temp dir (no network, no real agents). Keep it under 5s wall-clock — no real timeouts, no `sleep`.

subItems.title: Test drives the actual generated launcher script via bash and asserts the exact `pan tell` message for both pass and fail fixtures
Test isolates `claude` and `pan` via a temp PATH directory containing executable stubs — no real Anthropic API call, no real `pan` invocation, no real tmux
Test runs in under 5 seconds wall-clock and uses no real `sleep` / no real `timeout` triggers — the stub claude exits immediately
Test additionally covers the fallback path: when the temp PATH has no `pan` binary at all, the launcher signals REVIEWER_READY and the test asserts the warning line is present on the script's stderr
